channel.title: SSbusinessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbusinessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The big questions looming over OpenAI's trillion-dollar IPO
item.description: A public listing could be a major wealth event for Silicon Valley, but OpenAI's filing may reveal significant financial and existential risks.

item.title: Beyond the diploma: Skills that actually get graduates hired
item.description: Entry-level hiring rules have shifted, and experts at Fortune's Workplace Innovation Summit discussed the implications.

item.title: Barnes & Noble CEO clarifies stance on AI-written books after refusing to ban them: 'This is a straightforward rejection of AI books'
item.description: James Daunt told Fortune that deciding what counts as AI-generated is the publisher's responsibility, not the seller's.

item.title: Microsoft reports reveal AI's real cost problem: Using the tech is more expensive than paying human employees
item.description: Companies are rushing to encourage AI use, but some find that increased usage drives up costs.
